items.title: Japan manager adopts very old-fashioned method to give instructions to World Cup stars as fans say ‘you have to applaud’
items.summary: JAPAN boss Hajime Moriyasu was hailed for his old-school timekeeping methods during his side's dramatic 2-2 draw against the Netherlands. Samurai Blue twice battled from a goal down after Liverpool's Virgil van Dijk had put the Dutch ahead. Keito Nakamura then levelled it up before West Ham star Cry

items.title: 'Hard to read' Bellingham criticism - Henderson
items.summary: England's Jordan Henderson says he finds it "hard to read" what is written about Jude Bellingham but believes the midfielder can be the team's "X-factor" at the World Cup.

items.title: Japan leave it late and Germany’s magnificent seven: World Cup Daily – podcast
items.summary: Max Rushden is joined by Barry Glendenning , Alex Abnos and Ben Fisher as the Netherlands and Japan play out a cracker in Dallas, while Germany put seven past Curaçao Rate, review, share on Apple Podcasts and join the conversation on email . items.title: Wirtz, Isak & Diomande shine at World Cup as Iraola plots Liverpool revival
items.summary: Florian Wirtz, Alexander Isak and Yan Diomande all shine as World Cup provides perfect scouting mission for new Liverpool boss Andoni Iraola, says Phil McNulty.

items.title: 'Thunderbolt!' - Sweden's Ayari scores stunner but refuses to celebrate
items.summary: Yasin Ayari scores a stunner for Sweden against Tunisia but refuses to celebrate. The Brighton midfielder could have represented the opposition as his father is Tunisian.

items.title: Two-goal Yasin Ayari combines with Sweden stars to sweep aside error-prone Tunisia
items.summary: When they picture a messiah, few perhaps imagine a mild-mannered 51-year-old with a greying beard who used to play at left-back for York City. Certainly Chelsea and West Ham fans don’t. But the degree to which Graham Potter is loved in Sweden has to be seen to be believed. He was a down-on-his-luck

items.title: Sweden 5 Tunisia 1: Ayari scores two stunners with Isak and Gyokeres both on target in statement World Cup win
items.summary: THE big question for Sweden heading into this World Cup was whether their two star strikers could play as a duo. Manager Graham Potter got his answer with a 5-1 win over Tunisia in which Alexander Isak and Viktor Gyokeres both got on the scoresheet. Liverpool star Isak and Arsenal ace Gyokeres are m

items.title: Gyokeres and Isak shine as Sweden secure dominant win over Tunisia
items.summary: Alexander Isak and Viktor Gyokeres were in fine form as Sweden cruised to a comfortable 5-1 victory against Tunisia to kickstart their World Cup campaign in Group F.

items.title: Littler targeting trophy sweep after World Cup triumph with Humphries
items.summary: Luke Littler is aiming for a clean sweep of every major trophy in darts after his World Cup triumph with Luke Humphries.

items.title: Iraq head coach Graham Arnold: ‘We’re capable of doing something that will shock the world’
items.summary: Australian has had to contend with war, 50C heat and playoffs to steer country to a first World Cup in 40 years Twenty-eight months, 21 games, four rounds, a 117th-minute penalty and a playoff. A coach stuck in Dubai where he watches war start over the water, bombs shaking everything. A team trapped

items.title: France’s Adrien Rabiot: ‘We all have a role. You have to be humble with that’
items.summary: The midfielder on providing the balance to allow attacking stars to shine and wanting a fitting World Cup sign-off for Deschamps France will look a little different this summer. “Naturally, it seems a bit more attacking than usual,” Adrien Rabiot says. “I think it is good because we have the players
